zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Isabel de Bromley of Berkeley於1280年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾卡維斯瓦爾時，她的父親Richard de Bromley29歲，母親Elizabeth de Knocton50歲。她和Sir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizard至少有3個女兒。她去世於1326年在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊享年46歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bromley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places called Bromley, in Essex, Hertfordshire, Kent (now in Greater London), Greater London, Greater Manchester, Staffordshire, Shropshire, and elsewhere. Most are named with Old English brōm ‘broom’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’, but Bromley (near Bow) in Greater London is from Old English bræmbel ‘bramble’ + lēah.

variants.name: Brimley
meanings: habitational name, perhaps from Brimley in Devon or Brimbley in Stoke Abbott, Dorset, both named with Old English brōm ‘broom’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.
variant of   Bromley  .
English:

variants.name: Brumley
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Bromley  . This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Berkeley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or more of the numerous places so named, such as Berkeley in Gloucestershire, named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Scottish Barclay .
Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of   Berkowitz  .

variants.name: Barclay
meanings: Scottish: habitational name of English origin, from any of several places so called, such as Berkley in Somerset or Berkeley in Gloucestershire, both named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

History: The surname was brought to Scotland from southwestern England in the 12th century; Walter de Berchelai or Berkelai was Chamberlain of Scotland in 1165. It was long assumed that they were from the family that held Berkeley Castle in Gloucestershire, but the historian G. W. S. Barrow (1973, The Kingdom of the Scots) adduced evidence that they are more probably from Berkley in Somerset.

variants.name: Berkley
meanings: English: variant of   Berkeley  . Compare Scottish   Barclay  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): assimilated form of   Berkowitz  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Isabel de Bromley of Berkeley was born in 1280, in Caverswall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard de Bromley, was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth de Knocton, was 50. She had at least 3 daughters with Sir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizard. She died in 1326, in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Isabel de Bromley of Berkeley est née en 1280, à Caverswall, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard de Bromley, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Knocton, avait 50 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 filles avec Sir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizard. Elle est décédée en 1326, à Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Isabel de Bromley of Berkeley 1280 in Caverswall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard de Bromley 29 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth de Knocton 50. Sie hatte mit Sir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizard mindestens 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1326 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabel de Bromley of Berkeley è nata il nel 1280 nel Caverswall, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Richard de Bromley, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth de Knocton, aveva 50 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figlie con Sir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizard. Morì nel 1326, nel Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 46 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel de Bromley of Berkeleyが1280年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアカバースワルで生まれた時，父Richard de Bromleyは29歳，母Elizabeth de Knoctonは50歳であった。彼女はSir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizardとの間に少なくとも3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1326年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアバーケリーで46歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel de Bromley of Berkeley(이)가 1280년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 카버스월에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Bromley(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Knocton(은)는 50세였다. 그녀는 Sir John de Weston of Weston-Under-Lizard(와)과 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1326년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 버켈리에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Isabel de Bromley of Berkeley родилась в 1280 г. в местности кавирсвалл, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard de Bromley было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Knocton, 50.
